description: Suspended water droplets or ice crystals become a visible 'neparu' in the atmosphere. The noun claims only that visible suspension; shade and precipitation are possible results. 'nuphari' (fog) is cloud at or near the ground when it limits visibility.
articulatory_notes: Set the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and release nasal voice into middle-height front 'e'. Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and open into stressed central 'a'. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge for 'r', then finish with high-back rounded 'u'.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: A cloud changes at its edges even while a speaker treats it as one visible form. It offers a concrete case of continuity without fixed shape.

semantic_domains.nature: suspended atmospheric water or ice visible from the ground
semantic_domains.physical: a mass of droplets or crystals held in air
